title: Quararibea lasiocalyx
intro: Quararibea lasiocalyx, also known as the 'Cuchumbay' or 'Quararibea', is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been found to aid in treating conditions such as fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Quararibea lasiocalyx include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Quararibea lasiocalyx include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.

It belongs to the genus Quararibea, which consists of approximately 70 species of trees and shrubs. Quararibea lasiocalyx is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, specifically in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. This plant is part of the order Malpighiales and has been classified based on its morphological characteristics, including its distinctive leaves and flowers.
